name: Coordinated Inauthentic Behavior
description: Detection, conceptualization, and longitudinal study of coordinated link sharing and inauthentic networks on social media platforms. Anchors the CooRnet line of work and remains the researcher's signature contribution to disinformation studies.

name: Information Disorder & Problematic Content
description: Studies of misinformation, disinformation, hyperpartisan and low-quality news, and problematic health content circulating in hybrid media systems. Covers conceptual taxonomies of 'fake news' as well as empirical mappings across countries.

name: Platform Governance & Research Data Access
description: Critical analysis of platform policies (Meta content reduction, CrowdTangle shutdown, DSA-era APIs) and their consequences for what users see and what researchers can study. A growing current priority following the closure of legacy data pipelines.

name: Elections & Political Communication on Social Media
description: Empirical case studies of electoral campaigns on Facebook, Instagram and Twitter — Italian (2018, 2019, 2022), German (2021), Brazilian post-Bolsonaro and European elections — with attention to partisanship, polarization and campaign dynamics.

name: Italian News Ecosystem (MINE)
description: The institutional research line of the MINE programme: mapping Italian media coverage, partisan attention, news consumption and political parallelism. Umbrella theme uniting the PI's domestic empirical work since 2017.

name: Generative AI and Media
description: Emerging research and teaching agenda on generative AI as both an object of study (synthetic visuals in influence operations, AI in political economy) and a methodological instrument. Reflects new courses and 2026 publications.

name: LLMs for Text Classification & Annotation
description: Methodological work on integrating large language models into communication research pipelines: validation, embeddings, clustering of political news, and LLMs-in-the-loop workflows.

name: Computational Social Science Methods
description: Reflexive and methodological contributions on digital traces, social media APIs, Facebook ads-based survey recruitment, and the epistemology of big data for social research. Includes the long-standing 'open laboratory' agenda.

name: Digital Social Network Analysis
description: Network-analytic study of coordinated networks, hyperpartisan clusters and information diffusion on social platforms. Underpins both the CLSB toolchain and the PI's Master-level teaching.

name: Social TV & Audience Engagement
description: Earlier and ongoing line on second-screen practices, Twitter-TV audiences and participation around political talk shows and entertainment formats. Historical backbone of the PI's social media research.

name: Health Information & Public Health Communication
description: Studies of online health content, COVID-19 fact-checking, vaccine and pandemic narratives, and coordinated circulation of problematic health information in Italy, India and Nigeria.

name: Sociocybernetics & Social Systems Theory
description: Theoretical foundation grounded in Luhmannian systems theory and sociocybernetics, bridging communication theory and internet studies. A long-running conceptual register the PI returns to in framing pieces.

name: Twenty-Year Reflections on Social Media's Trajectory
description: Anniversary essays for Social Media + Society reflecting critically on how social media platforms have transformed since the early 2000s, lamenting losses of intimacy, community, and reciprocal sociality while critiquing the political economy of contemporary platforms.

name: TikTok's Algorithmic Culture and Contested Content
description: Studies examining TikTok specifically as a distinctive platform whose algorithmic affordances shape public discourse, content circulation, and the negotiation of contested or harmful content.

name: AI Red-Teaming as Sociotechnical Practice
description: Papers framing AI red-teaming and evaluation as sociotechnical activities requiring labor, value judgments, and public participation, rather than purely technical procedures.

name: Polarization, Deliberation, and Democratic Communication Online
description: Works addressing destructive polarization in online environments and evaluating normative or empirical approaches—deliberative ideals, depolarization recommendations, balanced exposure interventions—to counter it.
